message:
fix(fts): prevent spurious full FTS rebuild on every startup (#184)

Two bugs caused ensure_fts_consistency_via_frankensqlite() to trigger a
multi-hour FTS rebuild on every database open:

1. read_fts_franken_rebuild_generation() used .query() with a ?1
   placeholder but never bound the parameter — the unbound query always
   returned no rows, so the generation marker was never found.

   Fix: use query_row_map() with fparams![FTS_FRANKEN_REBUILD_META_KEY]
   to properly bind the key, matching the pattern used everywhere else
   in the file.

2. Even with the binding fixed, databases that predate the marker
   (or where a prior rebuild OOM'd before recording it) would still
   trigger a full rebuild.

   Fix: before triggering the expensive rebuild, probe whether
   fts_messages is already populated and consistent (>=90% of messages
   indexed). If so, just stamp the generation marker and skip the
   rebuild entirely.

On a 457K-message database this eliminates a multi-hour 100% CPU
rebuild that could OOM on machines with <8GB RAM.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
